P0-4: Aspirational vs operational clearly distinguished with Current State paragraph (lines 120-121) and maturity levels

P1-3: Evidence saturation named as B2 balancing loop
P1-4: Dev State and Maturity columns distinguish operational from aspirational

new_findings.title: Ship section conflates M0-M4 capability maturity with human delegation levels
new_findings.description: Lines 305-309 map code-push authority to mesh maturity (M0-M2, M3, M4), but shipping authority is a human delegation decision (L0-L5 in PHILOSOPHY.md), not a system capability metric. PHILOSOPHY.md line 107 explicitly states these two are orthogonal. M3 capability maturity of a subsystem does not automatically mean the human delegates to Level 3. The Ship section should reference the delegation ladder or explain how the two ladders interact for shipping decisions.

new_findings.section: The Capability Mesh > Current Mesh State
new_findings.title: Execution cell at M0 permanently caps system-level trust at M0
new_findings.description: The weakest-link formula (line 151) states system-level trust = min(maturity across mesh cells). Execution (Hassease + Codex) is at M0 (brainstorm/plan phase). This means the system's trust level is permanently M0 until Execution ships, regardless of how mature other subsystems become. Either: (a) Execution should be excluded from the min() calculation until it's past M0 (since M0 = 'not yet built'), (b) the weakest-link formula should apply only to M1+ cells, or (c) the document should acknowledge that the system is currently M0 by its own metric. Any of these is fine; the current text doesn't reconcile.

new_findings.title: Cost-per-landable-change baseline is stale
new_findings.description: Line 334 cites $1.17/landable change from 2026-02-28 as the baseline. Project memory records $2.93/landable change as of 2026-03-18 (785 sessions). The 2.5x increase is not necessarily bad (scope and quality may have expanded), but a reader encountering '$1.17' without the update will form an inaccurate picture. Either update to the current figure or add context: 'The initial baseline was $1.17 (2026-02-28); current baseline is $X.XX (2026-03-18) reflecting expanded review scope.'

new_findings.title: Phase 4 (adaptive routing) labeled as downstream of all upstream sources, but flywheel already operates on Interspect alone
new_findings.description: Lines 126-133 present a 4-phase dependency ordering where Phase 4 = 'Routing (Interspect with full evidence) -- needs all upstream sources.' But the Current State section (line 120) says the flywheel already operates on Interspect evidence alone. This creates a tension: is current Interspect routing Phase 4 or pre-Phase 1? The resolution is that current routing is 'static + complexity-aware' (not adaptive), and Phase 4 is 'adaptive routing.' But the label 'Routing (Interspect with full evidence)' doesn't distinguish these. Suggest: 'Phase 4 (convergence): Adaptive routing via Interspect with full evidence -- currently Interspect operates in limited mode with its own signals only.'

new_findings.title: Arcgentica citation specificity creates fragility
new_findings.description: Line 241 cites Symbolica AI's Arcgentica achieving '36% on ARC-AGI-3 at $1,005 total -- while raw Claude Opus 4.6 scored 0.25% at $8,900.' These are precise numbers from a specific benchmark run. If the numbers change (model updates, benchmark revisions), the citation becomes misleading. The structural argument (orchestration beats raw capability) is strong regardless of specific numbers. Consider: 'Symbolica AI's Arcgentica demonstrated >100x cost-efficiency improvement through orchestrator-delegates-to-sub-agents architecture on abstract reasoning benchmarks, outperforming raw frontier models.'

new_findings.title: Trust transfer probation period has no defined duration or criteria
new_findings.description: Lines 224-225 describe trust transfer for subsystem replacements: 'probationary access to the predecessor's maturity level with a verification period.' But neither the duration of probation nor the criteria for confirming/revoking inherited trust are specified. Compare: the Earn phase (lines 204-208) defines evidence type, time window, evaluating authority, and success threshold. The Trust Transfer section should reference these same dimensions. Without them, probation is undefined.

new_findings.title: Flywheel time constant not estimated even approximately
new_findings.description: The flywheel diagram shows the reinforcing loop but provides no estimate of revolution time. How long from 'sprint produces evidence' to 'routing improved by that evidence'? The evidence quarantine is 48 hours (line 368). Interspect proposals require manual approval at current maturity. A rough estimate (days? weeks? months?) would help readers assess whether the flywheel is a tight operational loop or a slow strategic accumulation. Even a range like '1-4 weeks per revolution for the Interspect-only loop' would ground the narrative.

new_findings.title: Interface evidence table lists signals but not collection status
new_findings.description: Lines 185-193 define 5 critical interface signals (entity identity agreement rate, attribution chain integrity, etc.), but unlike the Capability Mesh table, there is no column indicating which of these signals are currently measured vs aspirational. Given the prior review's P1-4 finding about hypothetical signals, the interface table should include the same shipped/aspirational distinction.

new_findings.title: Bead counts will be stale within days of publication
new_findings.description: Lines 362 and 373 cite '1,456 beads tracked, 1,239 closed'. Actual count is now 1,534/1,313. At the current pace (~2-3 beads/day), these numbers are stale within a week. Consider: 'As of April 2026 (>1,400 beads tracked, >1,200 closed)' to avoid precision-that-decays. Or add a 'last verified' date next to the specific numbers.

new_findings.title: OODARC lens absent from vision despite being the philosophical foundation
new_findings.description: PHILOSOPHY.md defines OODARC as the core lens for the flywheel ('authority -> actions -> evidence -> authority is an instance of OODARC'). The vision doc mentions OODARC only in passing (line 55, Skaffen description; line 82, OS description) but never connects it to the flywheel. This is arguably correct (the vision is for external audiences who don't need the philosophical vocabulary), but it means the flywheel section reinvents the OODARC framing without naming it. No action needed unless the audience changes.

improvements.title: Add a 'minimum viable mesh' definition: which cells must be M1+ for system-level trust to be meaningful
Add approximate time constants for each flywheel link (evidence quarantine: 48h, routing proposal: manual, maturity advancement: quarterly)
Add a trust bootstrap section: new subsystems at M0 have zero trust but need some operational authority to generate evidence
